lessons.title: Judicial Review — Grounds and Procedure
lessons.description: Judicial review is the supervisory jurisdiction of the High Court by which the legality of decisions, actions, and failures of public bodies — including legislation — is assessed.

lessons.title: Constitutional Dimension of Administrative Fairness
lessons.description: Once a court determines that a law or act is unconstitutional, what happens?

lessons.title: Remedies in Judicial Review
lessons.description: This is a distinctive feature of Irish constitutional law: the power of the President to refer a bill to the Supreme Court for a determination of its constitutionality before it is signed into law.
